docstring.text:
Scoring functionality for evaluating AI model responses across various dimensions
including harm detection, objective completion, and content classification.

A utility class for scoring prompts in batches in a parallelizable and convenient way.

This class provides functionality to score existing prompts stored in memory
without any target interaction, making it a pure scoring utility.
Score the responses that match the specified filters.

params.name: scorer
params.desc: The Scorer object to use for scoring.

params.name: attack_id
params.desc: The ID of the attack. Defaults to None.

params.name: conversation_id
params.desc: The ID of the conversation. Defaults to None.

params.name: prompt_ids
params.desc: A list of prompt IDs. Defaults to None.

params.name: labels
params.desc: A dictionary of labels. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_after
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent after this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: sent_before
params.desc: Filter for prompts sent before this datetime. Defaults to None.

params.name: original_values
params.desc: A list of original values. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_values
params.desc: A list of converted values. Defaults to None.

params.name: data_type
params.desc: The data type to filter by. Defaults to None.

params.name: not_data_type
params.desc: The data type to exclude. Defaults to None.

params.name: converted_value_sha256
params.desc:
A list of SHA256 hashes of converted values.
Defaults to None.

params.name: objective
params.desc:
A task is used to give the scorer more context on what exactly to score.
A task might be the request prompt text or the original attack model's objective.
**Note: the same task is applied to all matched prompts.** Defaults to an empty string.

returns.desc: list[Score]: A list of Score objects for responses that match the specified filters.

raises.desc: If no entries match the provided filters.

docstring.text: Initialize the BatchScorer.

params.name: batch_size
params.desc:
The (max) batch size for sending prompts. Defaults to 10.
Note: If using a scorer that takes a prompt target, and providing max requests per
minute on the target, this should be set to 1 to ensure proper rate limit management.

docstring.text:
Console printer for scorer information with enhanced formatting.

This printer formats scorer details for console display with optional color coding,
proper indentation, and visual hierarchy. Colors can be disabled for consoles
that don't support ANSI characters.

Print harm scorer information including type, nested scorers, and evaluation metrics.

This method displays:
- Scorer type and identity information
- Nested sub-scorers (for composite scorers)
- Harm evaluation metrics (MAE, Krippendorff alpha) from the registry

params.name: scorer_identifier
params.desc: The scorer identifier to print information for.

params.name: harm_category
params.desc: The harm category for looking up metrics (e.g., "hate_speech", "violence").

docstring.text:
Print objective scorer information including type, nested scorers, and evaluation metrics.

This method displays:
- Scorer type and identity information
- Nested sub-scorers (for composite scorers)
- Objective evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1) from the registry

params.name: scorer_identifier
params.desc: The scorer identifier to print information for.

docstring.text: Initialize the console scorer printer.

params.name: indent_size
params.desc:
Number of spaces for indentation. Must be non-negative.
Defaults to 2.

params.name: enable_colors
params.desc:
Whether to enable ANSI color output. When False,
all output will be plain text without colors. Defaults to True.

docstring.text:
Scorer that evaluates entire conversation history rather than individual messages.

This scorer wraps another scorer (FloatScaleScorer or TrueFalseScorer) and evaluates
the full conversation context. Useful for multi-turn conversations where context matters
(e.g., psychosocial harms that emerge over time or persuasion/deception over many messages).

The ConversationScorer dynamically inherits from the same base class as the wrapped scorer,
ensuring proper type compatibility.

Note: This class cannot be instantiated directly. Use create_conversation_scorer() factory instead.
Validate scores by delegating to the wrapped scorer's validation.

params.name: scores
params.desc: The scores to validate.

docstring.text: Abstract base class for scorers.

Evaluate this scorer against human-labeled datasets.

Uses file mapping to determine which datasets to evaluate and how to aggregate results.

params.name: file_mapping
params.desc:
Optional ScorerEvalDatasetFiles configuration.
If not provided, uses the scorer's configured evaluation_file_mapping.
Maps input file patterns to an output result file.

params.name: num_scorer_trials
params.desc: Number of times to score each response (for measuring variance). Defaults to 3.

params.name: update_registry_behavior
params.desc:
Controls how existing registry entries are handled.
- SKIP_IF_EXISTS (default): Check registry for existing results. If found, return cached metrics.
- ALWAYS_UPDATE: Always run evaluation and overwrite any existing registry entry.
- NEVER_UPDATE: Always run evaluation but never write to registry (for debugging).
Defaults to RegistryUpdateBehavior.SKIP_IF_EXISTS.

params.name: max_concurrency
params.desc: Maximum number of concurrent scoring requests. Defaults to 10.

returns.desc: The evaluation metrics, or None if no datasets found.

raises.desc: If no file_mapping is provided and no evaluation_file_mapping is configured.

docstring.text:
Compute a behavioral equivalence hash for evaluation grouping.

Delegates to ``ScorerEvaluationIdentifier`` which filters target children
(prompt_target, converter_target) to behavioral params only, so the same
scorer configuration on different deployments produces the same eval hash.

returns.desc: A hex-encoded SHA256 hash suitable for eval registry keying.

docstring.text:
Get evaluation metrics for this scorer from the configured evaluation result file.

Looks up metrics by this scorer's identity hash in the JSONL result file.
The result file may contain entries for multiple scorer configurations.

Subclasses must implement this to return the appropriate metrics type:
- TrueFalseScorer subclasses should return ObjectiveScorerMetrics
- FloatScaleScorer subclasses should return HarmScorerMetrics

returns.desc: The metrics for this scorer, or None if not found or not configured.

docstring.text: Scales a value from 0 to 1 based on the given min and max values. E.g. 3 stars out of 5 stars would be .5.

params.name: value
params.desc: The value to be scaled.

params.name: min_value
params.desc: The minimum value of the range.

params.name: max_value
params.desc: The maximum value of the range.

docstring.text: Score the message, add the results to the database, and return a list of Score objects.

params.name: message
params.desc: The message to be scored.

params.name: objective
params.desc:
The task or objective based on which the message should be scored.
Defaults to None.

params.name: role_filter
params.desc:
Only score messages with this exact stored role.
Use "assistant" to score only real assistant responses, or "simulated_assistant"
to score only simulated responses. Defaults to None (no filtering).

params.name: skip_on_error_result
params.desc: If True, skip scoring if the message contains an error. Defaults to False.

params.name: infer_objective_from_request
params.desc:
If True, infer the objective from the message's previous request
when objective is not provided. Defaults to False.

returns.desc: list[Score]: A list of Score objects representing the results.

raises.desc: If scoring raises a PyRIT exception (re-raised with enhanced context).
If scoring raises a non-PyRIT exception (wrapped with scorer context).

docstring.text: Score the given image using the chat target.

params.name: image_path
params.desc: The path to the image file to be scored.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective based on which the image should be scored. Defaults to None.

returns.desc: list[Score]: A list of Score objects representing the results.

docstring.text: Score a batch of images asynchronously.

params.name: image_paths
params.desc: Sequence of paths to image files to be scored.

params.name: objectives
params.desc:
Optional sequence of objectives corresponding to each image.
If provided, must match the length of image_paths. Defaults to None.

params.name: batch_size
params.desc: Maximum number of images to score concurrently. Defaults to 10.

returns.desc: list[Score]: A list of Score objects representing the scoring results for all images.

raises.desc: If the number of objectives does not match the number of image_paths.

docstring.text: Score multiple prompts in batches using the provided objectives.

params.name: messages
params.desc: The messages to be scored.

params.name: objectives
params.desc:
The objectives/tasks based on which the prompts should be scored.
Must have the same length as messages.

params.name: batch_size
params.desc: The maximum batch size for processing prompts. Defaults to 10.

params.name: role_filter
params.desc:
If provided, only score pieces with this role.
Defaults to None (no filtering).

params.name: skip_on_error_result
params.desc: If True, skip scoring pieces that have errors. Defaults to False.

params.name: infer_objective_from_request
params.desc:
If True and objective is empty, attempt to infer
the objective from the request. Defaults to False.

returns.desc: list[Score]: A flattened list of Score objects from all scored prompts.

raises.desc:
If objectives is empty or if the number of objectives doesn't match
the number of messages.

docstring.text: Score a response using an objective scorer and optional auxiliary scorers.

params.name: response
params.desc: Response containing pieces to score.

params.name: objective_scorer
params.desc: The main scorer to determine success. Defaults to None.

params.name: auxiliary_scorers
params.desc: List of auxiliary scorers to apply. Defaults to None.

params.name: role_filter
params.desc:
Only score pieces with this exact stored role.
Defaults to "assistant" (real responses only, not simulated).

params.name: objective
params.desc: Task/objective for scoring context. Defaults to None.

params.name: skip_on_error_result
params.desc: If True, skip scoring pieces that have errors. Defaults to True.

returns.desc:
Dict[str, List[Score]]: Dictionary with keys `auxiliary_scores` and `objective_scores`
containing lists of scores from each type of scorer.

docstring.text:
Score a response using multiple scorers in parallel.

This method applies each scorer to the first scorable response piece (filtered by role and error),
and returns all scores. This is typically used for auxiliary scoring where all results are needed.

params.name: response
params.desc: The response containing pieces to score.

params.name: scorers
params.desc: List of scorers to apply.

params.name: role_filter
params.desc:
Only score pieces with this exact stored role.
Defaults to "assistant" (real responses only, not simulated).

params.name: objective
params.desc: Optional objective description for scoring context.

params.name: skip_on_error_result
params.desc: If True, skip scoring pieces that have errors (default: True).

returns.desc: List[Score]: All scores from all scorers

docstring.text: Scores the given text based on the task using the chat target.

params.name: text
params.desc: The text to be scored.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The task based on which the text should be scored

returns.desc: list[Score]: A list of Score objects representing the results.

docstring.text:
Validate the scores returned by the scorer. Because some scorers may require
specific Score types or values.

params.name: scores
params.desc: The scores to be validated.

attributes.name: scorer_type
attributes.docstring:
Get the scorer type based on class hierarchy.

Returns:
    ScoreType: "true_false" for TrueFalseScorer subclasses,
              "float_scale" for FloatScaleScorer subclasses,
              "unknown" for other scorers.

docstring.text: Initialize the Scorer.

params.name: validator
params.desc: Validator for message pieces and scorer configuration.

docstring.text:
Abstract base class for printing scorer information.

This interface defines the contract for printing scorer details including
type information, nested sub-scorers, and evaluation metrics from the registry.
Implementations can render output to console, logs, files, or other outputs.

Print harm scorer information including type, nested scorers, and evaluation metrics.

This method displays:
- Scorer type and identity information
- Nested sub-scorers (for composite scorers)
- Harm evaluation metrics (MAE, Krippendorff alpha) from the registry

params.name: scorer_identifier
params.desc: The scorer identifier to print information for.

params.name: harm_category
params.desc: The harm category for looking up metrics (e.g., "hate_speech", "violence").

docstring.text:
Print objective scorer information including type, nested scorers, and evaluation metrics.

This method displays:
- Scorer type and identity information
- Nested sub-scorers (for composite scorers)
- Objective evaluation metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1) from the registry

params.name: scorer_identifier
params.desc: The scorer identifier to print information for.

docstring.text:
Validates message pieces and scorer configurations.

This class provides validation for scorer inputs, ensuring that message pieces meet
required criteria such as data types, roles, and metadata requirements.
Check if a message piece is supported by this validator.

params.name: message_piece
params.desc: The message piece to check.

returns.desc: True if the message piece meets all validation criteria, False otherwise.

docstring.text: Validate a message and objective against configured requirements.

params.name: message
params.desc: The message to validate.

params.name: objective
params.desc: The objective string, if required.

raises.desc: If validation fails due to unsupported pieces, exceeding max pieces, or missing objective.

docstring.text: Initialize the ScorerPromptValidator.

params.name: supported_data_types
params.desc:
Data types that the scorer supports.
Defaults to all data types if not provided.

params.name: required_metadata
params.desc:
Metadata keys that must be present in message pieces.
Defaults to empty list.

params.name: supported_roles
params.desc:
Message roles that the scorer supports.
Defaults to all roles if not provided.

params.name: max_pieces_in_response
params.desc:
Maximum number of pieces allowed in a response.
Defaults to None (no limit).

params.name: max_text_length
params.desc:
Maximum character length for text data type pieces.
Defaults to None (no limit).

params.name: enforce_all_pieces_valid
params.desc:
Whether all pieces must be valid or just at least one.
Defaults to False.

params.name: raise_on_no_valid_pieces
params.desc:
Whether to raise ValueError when no pieces are valid.
Defaults to False, allowing scorers to handle empty results gracefully (e.g., returning
False for blocked responses). Set to True to raise an exception instead.

params.name: is_objective_required
params.desc: Whether an objective must be provided for scoring. Defaults to False.

docstring.text:
Create a ConversationScorer that inherits from the same type as the wrapped scorer.

This factory dynamically creates a ConversationScorer class that inherits from the wrapped scorer's
base class (FloatScaleScorer or TrueFalseScorer), ensuring the returned scorer is an instance
of both ConversationScorer and the wrapped scorer's type.

params.name: scorer
params.desc:
The scorer to wrap for conversation-level evaluation.
Must be an instance of FloatScaleScorer or TrueFalseScorer.

params.name: validator
params.desc:
Optional validator override.
If not provided, uses the wrapped scorer's validator.

returns.desc: A ConversationScorer instance that is also an instance of the wrapped scorer's type.

raises.desc: If the scorer is not an instance of FloatScaleScorer or TrueFalseScorer.
